title: $67 Billion Hedge Fund Flags a Rare AI Chip Signal for Stock Markets
excerpt: Citadel's Scott Rubner flagged a rare signal in AI chip stocks after retail turned sellers. Here is what the pattern shows now.
content_html:
AI chip stocks have cooled fast. The SOXX fund, which tracks the semiconductor index, sits about 15.7% below its June high, and after a long run of dip-buying, retail traders have started selling.

That flip is the rare signal Scott Rubner, Head of Equity Derivatives Strategy at Citadel Securities, just flagged. One that has marked past selloff lows, or rather, local bottoms.

What Rubner Flagged

In a July investor note, Rubner said retail clients turned net sellers of chips on two down days, July 2 and July 7, as the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX), the benchmark for major chip makers, fell about 5%. Selling into a falling SOX is rare.

Note: We chart SOXX, the exchange-traded fund that tracks the SOX index, because the index itself cannot be traded.

Moreover, he counted only about eight such episodes over the past year. Nearly all arrived late in a selloff, just before chips bounced. That's the AI chip bottom thesis this piece chases.

Citadel sees this through payment for order flow, the arrangement that lets it handle retail trades and read their positioning. That data is not easily accessible.

Why We Rebuilt the AI Chip Signal

Because that order flow is private, we rebuilt the signal from public data. Our proprietary Retail Capitulation Radar (RCR) tracks two leveraged chip funds, SOXL and SOXS, which aim to move two or three times the semiconductor index each day.

Retail traders dominate them. The RCR is our own bottom signal detector.

When retail dumps the bullish fund or crowds into the bearish one as chips drop, the behavior shows up in that trading. On the test, the strict signal fired twice, both in early March 2026.

The chart shows why that matters. SOXX has dropped about 16% from its June high, yet it still trades roughly 80% above that March base, where the signal last fired.

Here is the honest part. Citadel counted eight episodes, yet the public proxy (our metric) confirmed only two, and it did not reproduce the exact July signal on the chart. That gap cuts both ways. Either our proxy runs too tightly, or public data missed what Citadel's private order book saw.

Another Historical Pattern Shows Similarity

Still, both datasets point the same way. In Rubner's retail-selling episodes since February, chips rose over the next five to ten days every time, with a median gain near 18% over ten days, and the March case rose about 29%.

The proprietary radar above is deliberately strict, which is why it fired only twice. So we also ran a second, loser test that flags any two-day drop with broad chip weakness. That wider net catches more cases, ten in all, and it broadly agrees, with a median gain near 7% over the next ten days.

content_html_zh:
AI芯片股迅速降温。 追踪半导体指数的 SOXX 基金目前较 6 月高点低约 15.7%，经过长期逢低买入后，零售交易商已开始抛售。

这种转变是 Citadel 证券股票衍生品策略主管 Scott Rubner 刚刚指出的罕见信号。 它标志着过去的抛售低点，或者更确切地说，局部底部。

鲁布纳标记了什么

鲁布纳在 7 月份的一份投资者报告中表示，零售客户在 7 月 2 日和 7 月 7 日这两个下跌日中转向了芯片净卖家，因为主要芯片制造商的基准费城半导体指数 (SOX) 下跌了约 5%。 在 SOX 指数下跌的情况下出售股票的情况很少见。

注：我们绘制了跟踪 SOX 指数的交易所交易基金 SOXX 图表，因为该指数本身无法交易。

此外，据他统计，去年此类事件大约只有八起。 几乎所有股票都在抛售后期到来，就在筹码反弹之前。 这就是本文所追求的人工智能芯片底层论文。

Citadel 通过订单流支付看到了这一点，这种安排可以让它处理零售交易并读取其定位。 该数据不容易获取。

为什么我们要重建AI芯片信号

由于该订单流是私有的，因此我们根据公共数据重建了信号。 我们专有的零售投降雷达 (RCR) 跟踪两只杠杆芯片基金 SOXL 和 SOXS，其目标是每天波动半导体指数的两到三倍。

零售交易商在其中占据主导地位。 RCR 是我们自己的底部信号检测器。

当散户在筹码下跌时抛售看涨基金或涌入看跌基金时，这种行为就会在交易中显现出来。 在测试中，严格信号发射了两次，均是在 2026 年 3 月上旬。

该图表显示了为什么这很重要。 SOXX 已较 6 月高点下跌约 16%，但仍较上次发出信号的 3 月基点高出约 80%。

这是诚实的部分。 Citadel 统计了八集，但公共代理（我们的指标）只确认了两集，而且它没有在图表上重现 7 月的确切信号。 这种差距是双向的。 要么是我们的代理运行得太紧，要么是公共数据错过了 Citadel 的私人订单簿所看到的内容。

另一个历史模式显示出相似之处

尽管如此，两个数据集都指向相同的方向。 在鲁布纳2月份以来的散户抛售事件中，筹码每次都会在接下来的五到十天内上涨，十天内涨幅中位数接近18%，3月份的情况上涨约29%。

上面的专有雷达刻意严格，这就是为什么它只发射了两次。 因此，我们还进行了第二次失败者测试，以标记筹码广泛疲软的任何两天下跌。 这张更广泛的网捕获了更多病例，总共有 10 例，而且人们普遍同意，未来 10 天内的中值增长接近 7%。
